CONFIDENTIAL
SHAREHOLDER LETTER

Q1 2026

Patrick Madden, CEO & Founder · Company Confidential

Letter from CEO

To our new partners: Welcome to the fight. To our existing shareholders: Thank you for your continued belief in the inevitability of this mission.

We founded Deus X Defense on a single, contrarian observation: The physical world is soft. While the digital realm has been hardened by trillions of dollars in cybersecurity spend, our physical infrastructure—power grids, borders, schools, and stadiums—remains protected by a model that hasn't changed in fifty years: unarmed guards, analog cameras, and hope.

Deus X Defense is not a "drone company" or a "guarding company." We are building the Security Immune System — a unified platform that combines human judgment with autonomous speed.

The Engine: Man + Machine > Men

The core of our business is an arbitrage on efficiency. The "Cold Start" problem kills most robotics companies — they burn millions trying to sell robots to customers who don't know how to use them. We bypassed this by becoming the operator.

The formula is simple: we take low-margin manned guarding revenue (~7% EBITDA), layer in autonomous leverage via PUPPETMASTER C2, and output high-margin tech revenue (>30% GM, scaling toward 50-60%). The guard force is the distribution channel. The technology is the margin engine.

The Capital Reality

Our original capital strategy was conservative. The market refused to let us move slowly. The demand from major enterprise partners and the LAPD is not for "consulting" — it is for immediate, full-stack as-a-service programs.

In addition to closing the remaining $3.5M Seed Capital, we are opening a $500K/mo Strategic Bridge to finance three critical engines: Sales & Engineering Capacity, Product Hardening, and Inventory Financing.

The Moat: We Do Hard Things

Competitors sell components. Deus X delivers outcomes. Our moat is built on four layers of friction that compound over time:

Regulatory Friction: Our nationwide BVLOS waiver at 400ft is one of fewer than five ever issued. It took 18 months to earn. Operational Friction: CJIS-certified law enforcement officers in our operator pool — you can't hire this off the street. Deployment Friction: Site-specific training, works in the mud, not just the demo room. Integration Friction: Our Accenture partnership gives us access to 8,000+ enterprise clients through their existing trust relationships.

Operational Breakout

LAPD × World Cup 2026: The Deputy Chief ended our pitch early to state: "I've seen enough. We need you… If you're successful, we'll parade you around the world." This isn't a pilot — it's the public sector standard being set.

Accenture: We're moving clients from Security Budget to Capital Plan. Access to 8,000+ clients. This is distribution at a scale no drone company has ever achieved.

Orcrist Exclusivity: The only commercially available hard-kill cUAS interceptor at a $30K price point. We hold exclusive global commercial distribution rights.

Financial & M&A

TTM revenue is ~$25M across the combined entity. Qualified pipeline exceeds $105M with a sub-6-month close horizon — and no dedicated sales team. All new contracts carry >30% gross margin. Our 2026 base case projects ~$73M in revenue with a target of ~$1M+ monthly free cash flow by Q4'26 exit rate.

On M&A: Cascadia and Holdfast are closed. Invictus is in final diligence. Each acquisition adds revenue, geographic reach, and guard force for our DaaS upsell engine.

Product Roadmap

Now — PUPPETMASTER: The Engine. Live April. Single pane of glass C2 that unifies guards, drones, sensors, and cUAS into one command surface.

Next — Deus Halo: The Spear. Deploying with LAPD. Integrated air defense system with hard-kill cUAS capability via Orcrist.

Future — The Immune System: The Moat. Full autonomy. End-of-year pilot target. When the system learns, responds, and heals itself.

We built a business that works today to fund the autonomy of tomorrow.

Patrick Madden

CEO & Founder, Deus X Defense

The Security Immune System
Defend Our Way of Life
Confidential — For Qualified Investors Only
April 2026
Patrick Madden, Founder & CEO
The Threat

On March 1st, Iranian drones struck three data centers in the Gulf — the first kinetic attacks on data centers in history.

Iran has threatened the Stargate facilities our customer is building. $2,000 drones shut down billion-dollar shipping lanes.

Threat isn't theoretical. It's here.

That was overseas.
Now it's here.

CISA / FBI / NSA Joint Advisory AA24-038A
China is pre-positioned inside U.S. critical infrastructure.
  • ▸ Active implants in U.S. systems since at least 2021
  • ▸ Target: disrupt mobilization in a Taiwan contingency
  • ▸ Not espionage — pre-positioning for kinetic effect
CISA Advisory AA23-335A ∙ Nov 2023
Iran breached American water systems.
  • ▸ IRGC-affiliated group seized programmable logic controllers
  • ▸ 11+ U.S. water utilities confirmed compromised
  • ▸ Municipal OT systems — no meaningful perimeter
DOE OE-417 ∙ WECC 2025 Reliability Assessment
Physical attacks on the grid doubled in one year.
  • ▸ Coordinated sabotage of substations and transformers
  • ▸ 220 reported incidents in 2025 alone
  • ▸ Utilities have no armed response authority

“I do solemnly swear to support and defend the Constitution of the United States against all enemies, foreign and domestic.”

— Oath of Office, U.S. Armed Forces

And the industry's defense?

76%
Of organizations were hit by insider attacks in 2024 — up from 66% in 2019. You can't post a guard against an employee with a badge.
Cybersecurity Insiders 2024 Insider Threat Report
20 min
After 20 minutes of monitoring, security operator attention falls below acceptable levels
Sandia National Laboratories / U.S. DOE
97%
Of security alarms are false — resources wasted, real threats missed
LAPD verified data
40%+
Of U.S. cities won't guarantee police response to unverified alarms
Deep Sentinel analysis
100–300%
Annual security guard turnover. The workforce replaces itself every ~4 months.
BLS JOLTS 2024 / Security Industry Association

“Private operators have not supported security guards to the degree warranted by the facilities they protect.”

— Congressional Research Service, “Guarding America”

When the threat is inside, the model can’t be reformed — it has to be replaced.

The Vision

SOVEREIGN OS

Immune System for Critical Assets
Each module ships as a point product, generates revenue, and compounds when connected.
Cortex sees. Puppetmaster protects. Command proves. Sentinel learns — and the system compounds every shift.

03 The Wedge · Every Contract Compounds
1 Get In
Win a manned guarding or DaaS contract at a critical site.
2 Prove Value
Deliver excellence; collect behavioral data that trains SENTINEL.
3 Layer On
Stack DaaS on guarding, sensors on DaaS, cUAS where justified.
4 Cross-Sell
Hardware, SENTINEL-trained autonomy, COMMAND compliance.
5 Sovereign OS Destination
Full platform customer. Every new module lifts every other.
Why We Acquired
Security Is a Trust Industry.
We Earned the Right to Be Here.
You can't cold-call a nuclear plant with a drone. CI operators buy from the team already inside the building. We solved two structural problems with two acquisitions.
01
The Foothold.
Active contracts, security clearances, and the right to cross-sell on sites where we're already the trusted provider. The 8% margin guarding contract is the cost of admission.
02
The Training Data Engine.
450+ operators generate labeled training data every shift — every dispatch, escalation, and dismissed alert trains SENTINEL. This corpus cannot be purchased or synthesized.
The Data Engine
Operators Train the Machine.
The Machine Trains the Operator.
Knowing "there's a person" is easy. Knowing "that person doesn't belong here at 2am near the transformer yard" requires security domain expertise.
Step 01
PuppetMaster Recommends
AI fuses sensor data across the site and proposes an action — dispatch, escalate, dismiss.
AI PROPOSAL
Step 02
Operator Decides
Human approves, overrides, escalates, or dismisses — judgment in the loop.
HUMAN JUDGMENT
Step 03
SENTINEL Records
Decision, context, sensor data captured as labeled training signal.
STRUCTURED DATA
Step 04
Model Improves
Accuracy compounds with volume. Site-specific models learn local patterns.
AI COMPOUNDS
Step 05
Operator Validates
Human confirms improved recommendations. Catches edge cases. Cycle repeats every shift.
RLHF LOOP CLOSES
Every shift, every site, every decision — the loop compounds
Deploying, training, operationalizing, and maintaining Physical AI systems is hard. We have the people.
Operate Drone Fleets
Care, maintenance, pre-flight, mission planning.
Validate AI Alerts
Human-in-the-loop for regulators and customers.
Ground Truth for Edge Cases
Labeled data for situations the model hasn't seen.
Physical Response
When autonomy finds a real threat, humans intervene.
"The operators aren't a cost center. They train the models, care for the fleet, maintain the systems, and collaborate with the AI."

Thesis Validation
Three Theses. Eight Live Deals. All Executing in Q1–Q2 2026.
Thesis 1
3 Live Deals
Integrated Security Wins CI Incumbents
"If you can see what the guard can't, you win the building."
  • ONEOK: $1M ARR → expanding to ~$12.5M (DaaS + cUAS). Driven by performance.
  • LADWP: Unsolicited RFP. AI-enabled integrated physical security. Referred by LAPD.
  • Confidential O&G: Guard caught sleeping. Client terminating incumbent. Bid: ~$15–20M/yr manned + $5–10M DaaS.
Thesis 2
2 Live Deals
Win the Executive, Win the Enterprise
"The UHNW client is the trust gateway to the institutional contract."
  • Caris Life Sciences: Won founder's private golf course → $500K ARR home+golf → ~$1M ARR → bid invited on full HQ Immune System.
  • M6 Global + Chertoff: EP channel established as enterprise entry vector.
Thesis 3
3 Live Deals
Win the Air, Win the Building
"Drone-first-responder is the Trojan horse. The platform is what follows."
  • Dallas ISD: Sole-source $7.5M DFR procurement in development. $250M bond (Q3 vote). Architecting $6.2B bond for next-gen schools.
  • Stargate: ~$35K/mo DaaS. Client requested full cUAS before signing.
  • LAPD Phase 1: Accenture-teamed. 3-phase, 8-month roadmap. DFR + RTCC + cUAS + C2.
Combined near-term pipeline: $50M+ in identified contract value — exclusive of Accenture channel, cUAS sovereign, and bond-funded public sector.
Designed In
We're Being Written Into the Security Specifications Before Concrete Is Poured.
Schools — Dallas ISD
Writing the Playbook for 130,000 K-12 Schools
  • DxD authoring security specs for 26 new schools
  • Sovereign OS designed into blueprint from day one
  • 95% of existing schools run compatible hardware
  • $6.2B bond on May 2 ballot for next-gen schools
Data Centers — Stargate
Designed Into the Hyperscaler Ecosystem
  • Introduced via Related Digital (Stargate developer)
  • $7B Michigan facility (1 GW, OpenAI/Oracle, 2026 construction)
  • Three-party solution: DxD + Odin Labs + PDW
  • Iran has explicitly threatened Stargate facilities
Nuclear — Elementl Power + GNSP
First-Mover in Nuclear SMR Security
  • Finalizing MOU with Elementl (Google-backed nuclear partner)
  • 1.8 GW across 3 U.S. sites, backed by Energy Impact Partners ($4B+ AUM)
  • Confidential Top 3 hyperscaler provided multi-million dollar modular data center to accelerate development

Confidential Ohio facility — sourced via Elementl
Expand
Powering one of the world's largest data centers
Standard
GNSP bringing multiple confidential global nuclear facilities
Catalyst
Lack capacity to pursue — opportunity to set global standard
Three verticals. One pattern. That's not a sales motion — that's a standard.
3 verticals being designed into infrastructure
1 / 4
Appendix A1 · Thesis → Traction
We Planned to Start Small.
The Market Had Other Plans.
We expected training, consulting, and MVP iteration. Instead, massive deals came inbound — longer lead times, larger working capital burdens, and all attention consumed.
Accenture
Signed GTM partner. Investing to accelerate our tech stack — they need us to keep pace with them.
Signed
Deloitte
Reached out inbound. Asked us to take over their drone training school and be their operating partner for World Cup 2026.
Inbound
Multiple Hyperscalers
Leveraging relationships with multiple hyperscalers. Early-phase conversations.
Early Phase
Orcrist cUAS
$30K MSRP, 50% GM — massive commercial differentiator. Drone & robotics inventory hard to come by.
Differentiator
The $105M pipeline is DaaS only — excludes Accenture, Deloitte, and all cUAS. 3 commission-only reps. No proactive sales force.
Smaller deals are sliding into the pipe but we don't have the team to convert them.
Momentum is accelerating. Deal sizes are large. Inventory needs lead time.
Capital is needed to keep up with demand and de-risk — not to prove the thesis.
Every major deal inbound or through a partner
2 / 4
Appendix A1 · The Bottleneck
The Risk Isn't Demand.
It's Capacity.
Inventory
Drone inventory requires pre-orders months ahead of contract close. Don't have the cash to order ahead for the size of these deals.
Deal Teams
A normal Accenture deal team for a major account like LAPD requires major investment upfront.
Pipeline
1,263 deals. 3 commission-only reps. Can't handle multiple deals at once due to size, scale, and demand.
Gross Profit is positive. We're investing all the cash we have — and more — into R&D and solutions engineering to keep up with the pipeline.
The bottleneck isn't demand — it's capacity to capture it
3 / 4
Appendix A1 · Capital Structure
Three Tiers to
Capture the Full Opportunity.
Tier 01
Working Capital
Line of credit. Cash flow is tight. Major deals are coming — we need working capital to do the deals.
Fund inventory. Bridge deal-team ramp. De-risk lead times.
Tier 02
Scale
R&D + solutions engineers + BD/sales/partnerships. Scale the teams that build the product and convert the pipeline.
Convert $105M+ pipeline. Staff deployments. Build proactive sales force.
Tier 03
IP & Technology
Acquiring IP and technology to accelerate the vision. We're done with the foundation.
Compress the roadmap. Acquire tech assets that accelerate delivery.

CONFIDENTIAL INVESTMENT OVERVIEW · APRIL 2026

DEUS X DEFENSE

Sovereign OS — The First Integrated Security Immune System for the $142B Physical Infrastructure Market.

Deus X Defense is building the operating system for critical infrastructure protection. Sovereign OS connects elite human operators, autonomous robotics, and AI-native compliance into a single, unified security immune system — purpose-built to protect America’s most essential assets.

Patrick Madden, CEO & Founder · pat@deusxdefense.com · Only shared under NDA

DEFEND OUR WAY OF LIFE
CONFIDENTIAL — ONLY SHARED UNDER NDA
SCROLL
THE VISION

This Is Deus X Defense

We joined after 9/11 to stop fear from dictating our lives, yet that same brand of terror now haunts our streets. Our oath to defend against all enemies, foreign and domestic, is absolute: the battlefield has shifted, and the threat is home.

“Where violence is unsexy. Where autonomous systems, governed by human judgment, stand ready.”

INVESTMENT OPPORTUNITY

The Case Is Clear

Infrastructure is the new frontline. Market risk is retired by federal mandate. Invention risk is bypassed by strategic integration. The challenge is pure execution—and we are the only team built to win.

01
The Need Is Unquestioned
  • China pre-positioned inside US CI for 5+ years undetected. Volt Typhoon walked through the physical gaps that $213B/year in cyber spending left wide open. Confirmed by CISA, FBI, NSA & Microsoft.
  • Cyber is saturated. Physical AI is soft. $213B/yr cyber spend. Physical security: $142B at 6.8% CAGR.
  • CIRCIA May 2026 — 72-hr mandatory reporting, 300K+ entities. SAFER SKIES Act: $500M cUAS grants. No vendor closes the detect → respond → report loop.
  • $105M qualified pipeline, sub-6 months, no sales team. 10–20× industry benchmark. The market found us.
02
We Have an Unfair Advantage
  • The team that built this category. Pat led Axon’s move into tactical drones, robotics + TASER drone turret. Nick Gilmour built DFR at Skydio. Rob Robertson + Ross Stinson shaped Part 107 — they’re why we have the only nationwide BVLOS waiver at 400ft.
  • Multiple compounding moats are being created; Nationwide BVLOS waiver up to 400ft, Orcrist exclusive: $30K kinetic cUAS vs. $126K minimum elsewhere, multiple IP acquisitions in-process, and unmatched distribution partnerships in-progress.
  • Recognized World-Class Expertise. Accenture + Deloitte are coming to us — Physical Security is becoming a crisis and very few know physical security and how to deploy, integrate, and operate drones and robotics at scale.
03
The Thesis Is Playing Out
  • Development Partnerships w/ Sector Leaders. Finalizing development and launch partnerships with LAPD, Top 3 Data Center Developer, Top 3 Energy Generation & Utility Provider, Caris Life Sciences HQ.ACTIVE
  • Cross-sell proof: Confidential Energy Major — $1M ARR manned guarding. Now requesting drones + robots. Zero new prospecting.ACTIVE
  • Point product → full platform: Hyperscale Partner entered with DFR. Already requesting cUAS + full solution integration.ACTIVE
  • ~$25M acquired revenue, cash flowing from day one. Confidential O&G Major invited DxD to bid $15–20M manned + $5–10M DaaS.LIVE
04
Scale Mechanisms Are in Place
  • Acquisition Flywheel. Confidential Top 3 Energy Producer & Pipeline Operator requesting bid to expand existing manned guarding contract from ~$1M ARR to >$12M.LIVE
  • Lighthouse Effect. LAPD → Unsolicited LADWP RFP → World Cup → Olympics is in motion w/ intent to launch full system prior to Olympics across LAPD critical infrastructure.ACTIVE
  • Land & Expand. Submitting single source for $7.5M Dallas Independent School District (DISD) Drone-as-a-first responder contract, asked to help write RFP for fully integrated sensor network and advanced autonomy for $250M bond in Q3 and to help shape bond and security technology for $6Bn bond for new schools in 2027.ACTIVE
  • Major Distribution Unlocked. Accenture Physical Security & Drone/Robotic Operations partner for F500 and Deloitte initial discussions for same GTM and to take over their drone training school house.ACTIVE

“This is not a vision deck. This is a company that is already operating, already seeing massive demand, less than a quarter away from real MVPs, and already building the scale mechanisms needed to win.”

ORIGIN STORY

Why We Exist

Deus X isn’t a ‘founder story’ about financial opportunity or building an addictive app; it is an obsession born from a life spent protecting this nation. It is the result of hard-won lessons and direct experience in the public eye on what it actually takes to protect life and secure our future. This is the fulfillment of an oath to defend our way of life, forged by experience and driven by a vision that cannot wait.

Pat Madden
Founder & CEO · Deus X Defense
  • Founder & CEO, Deus X Defense (Oct 2025–Present)
  • SVP & GM, TASER and Robotics — Axon — Led TASER P&L, Robotics, New Ventures, HW Engineering while leading strategies around tactical drones & robotics, robotic security, and the launch of TASER 10 (2020–2025)
  • VP & Chief of Staff to President & CEO — Axon (2018–2020)
  • Engagement Manager — McKinsey & Company
  • U.S. Marine Force Recon Officer
  • Wharton MBA — University of Pennsylvania
“The time to act is now.”
2010 The Mission

The Weight of Command. In Afghanistan, my platoon was pinned down in a high-stress ambush. I called in air support on a compound to save my men; the next morning, the New York Times reported that we had killed 52 women and children. Whether that report was accurate or not, the burden was mine. That weight—the reality that a split-second decision in a “fog of war” moment can lead to irreversible tragedy—is why I joined Axon. I joined because of Rick Smith’s vision: a world where violence and killing weren’t necessary.

THE NEW YORK TIMES  •  2010

KABUL, Afghanistan — Afghan officials said Monday that 52 people were killed in southern Afghanistan on Friday when a rocket fired by coalition forces slammed into a house where women and children had taken shelter from fighting between NATO troops and militants. But American officials disputed the account.

Source: The New York Times · 2010 · Filter: grayscale
2018 The Motivation

Shortly after joining Axon, I met the Las Vegas Metro Police Chief in the aftermath of Mandalay Bay. When hearing about the tragedy and response, I asked what I thought was a naive question: what if we put a TASER on a drone and got it in the window in under a minute? The answer stopped me cold: “Over 50 lives would have been saved.” I became obsessed with how technology could be used to stop mass terror and violence.

2020 The Opportunity
■ THE 60-SECOND MANDATE
0:60
RESPONSE WINDOW · SECONDS

In 2020 I took over TASER with one core thesis: we could save lives by using robotics, drones, and autonomous AI to take humans away from the point of threat. Identify, confirm, and stop a threat in under 30 seconds — and make violence unsexy. We immediately went to work.

“It’s not enough to wait for a good guy with a gun to chase a bad guy with a gun, we need to stop the threat before the violence can spread.”
2022 The Announcement & The Lesson

By 2022 we had working prototypes. Pilots at the Waste Management Open and Arizona State University. Then Uvalde happened — we watched it unfold live. We made the decision: we couldn’t sit on the sidelines anymore. We announced.

The response was swift. Reuters, the press, ethics boards — the backlash was real. Nine of Axon’s twelve ethics board members resigned in protest. I went to work understanding the concerns.

Reuters / NPR / NYT — June 2022
“Taser maker halts drone project; most of its ethics panel resigns”

What I learned:

  • Private security needed to step up
  • Communities were skeptical of Big Brother
  • Critical Infrastructure providers wanted to control response and couldn’t wait on law enforcement
  • Law enforcement was critically understaffed and couldn’t support the response time needed

The answer wasn’t just technology — it was responsible operators, coordinating with law enforcement, integrated into a unified system that was capable of detecting, responding, confirming, and stopping/deterring a threat faster than a call for service.

2024 The Clarity

After years working with the French Ministry of Interior to help them adopt robotic security at the Paris Olympics, we kept hearing the same thing: too hard, regulation is complex, training is a problem. This was the Innovator’s Dilemma. The market didn’t need another point product — it needed a whole-product solution. An integrated platform. An OS.

“We’re on the precipice of an entirely new technology foundation. The way companies will win is by bringing that to their customers holistically.”— Head of Global Internet Investment Banking, Morgan Stanley
2025 Done Waiting

I watched the Florida State University shooting unfold — a campus where Parkland survivors had sought refuge, only to face a second shooting. That was it. The integrators, the OEMs, the legacy security companies — none of them had it in their blood. It was going to take too long.

In 2025 I stopped waiting. I assembled the best people I could find — operators, technologists, regulators, warriors — and built it myself.

“Allied will be ready in 5 years. We don’t have 5 years.”
■ THE OATH
“I do solemnly swear to support and defend the constitution of the United States against all enemies foreign and domestic.”
The Problem

The Physical World Is Soft

We founded Deus X on a single, contrarian observation.
The physical world is soft.

While the digital realm has been hardened by trillions in cybersecurity spend, our physical infrastructure — power grids, water systems, data centers, stadiums — remains protected by a model unchanged for fifty years: unarmed guards, analog cameras, and hope. That model is collapsing under the weight of forces that are only accelerating.

$213B
Spent on cyber in 2025
The Investment Gap

Protection Is Unbalanced

$213 billion spent on cybersecurity in 2025 — growing 12% per year. Twenty years of digital hardening. Meanwhile, physical security incidents cost organizations $1 trillion annually, and the infrastructure protecting our power grids, water systems, and data centers still runs on analog cameras and understaffed guards.

Newmark Security Report · 2025
“Organisations continue to invest heavily in cybersecurity while leaving front-line physical barriers dangerously thin.”
$213BCyber spend 2025
$1TAnnual physical security incidents cost
50 yrsUnchanged physical security model
5+
Years undetected inside US CI
ADVISORY: Volt Typhoon PRC Actors Active in U.S. Power Grid (CISA AA24-038A)
Volt Typhoon · CISA / FBI / NSA Joint Advisory

Our Enemies Exposed These Gaps

Volt Typhoon didn’t just hack networks — they used physical gaps in our infrastructure as entry points. Unprotected routers, unsecured access points, and facilities with no integrated sensor layer gave them a pathway into energy, water, and communications systems. They were inside for five years because nobody was watching the physical layer. Cyber hardening alone was never enough.

CISA Director Jen Easterly · Congressional Testimony, Jan. 2024
“This threat is not theoretical… what we’ve found to date is likely the tip of the iceberg. We are acting now, knowing that this threat is both real and urgent.”
5yr+Inside US systems undetected
16CI sectors targeted
0Physical response capability
■ CONFIRMED KINETIC ATTACK — MARCH 2026
INTEL ALERT // MARCH 2026: Iranian Shahed-136 Drones Strike AWS Data Center cooling arrays in UAE and Bahrain. Kinetic attack confirmed.
Classified Source · March 2026
Active Conflict · Middle East → Home

Our Adversaries Identified Our Achilles Heel

Iran struck Kuwait’s water and power plant. Attacked Israel’s oil refinery. Threatened Gulf desalination plants. Cartel-linked drone operators trained in Ukraine brought fiber-optic FPV drone tactics back to the US border. The FBI warned California law enforcement of potential Iranian UAV strikes on West Coast CI. The battlefield has come home.

CSIS Analysis · 2026
“State and non-state actors have identified critical infrastructure as target number one — because eliminating water, power, or communications does more damage than any conventional attack.”
KuwaitWater + power plant struck
West CoastFBI UAV threat warning
Mar 2026US-Iran conflict escalation
60,000
Cartel drone flights · H2 2024
Active · DHS Data

The Threat Is Already Here

Over 60,000 cartel drone flights along the southern border in the second half of 2024 alone. More than 1,000 drone incursions per month. Explosive-laden drones. Fentanyl deliveries. Cartel operatives trained in Ukraine bringing combat drone tactics to American soil. DHS testimony: “It’s only a matter of time before Americans or law enforcement become targets.”

Reuters / Pentagon · Feb 2026
“The adversaries have 17 times the number of drones, twice the amount of flight hours, and unlimited funding to grow their operations.”
60KDrone flights H2 2024
1,000+Monthly border incursions
17×Adversary drone advantage
300K+
Covered entities · CIRCIA
Effective May 2026 · Federal Mandate

Regulation Is Forcing The Hand

CIRCIA: mandatory 72-hour cyber incident reporting for all 16 CI sectors. 300,000+ covered entities. Federal enforcement teeth. 50% of all 2025 ransomware attacks targeted critical infrastructure — up 34% year-over-year. No existing vendor automates the full detect → respond → report loop. The compliance clock is running.

KELA Research · 2025
“With 50% of ransomware attacks targeting critical infrastructure, the message is clear: cyber resilience is now a matter of national defense.”
300K+Entities covered by CIRCIA
72hrMandatory reporting window
+34%YoY CI attacks 2025

“When we talk about homeland defense, we’re not just talking about military bases, power grids, and ports — we’re talking about places where Americans gather. Whether it’s a forward operating base, an outdoor concert venue, or a stadium hosting the World Cup, the principles of risk assessment and physical protection remain the same.”

Army Brig. Gen. Matt Ross, Director — Joint Interagency Task Force 401, Pentagon Counter-Drone Command
Why Now

Why 2026, Not 2020

Why couldn’t this company exist in 2020? Because none of the seven forces below did. Between 2023 and 2026, all of them inverted. The threats above were already waiting. The window is twenty-four months wide.

01 2024–2026 · Critical Infrastructure Capex

The Targets Got Bigger and the Hyperscalers Are Paying

Hyperscaler 2026 capex is targeting roughly $700 billion across Microsoft, Google, Amazon, Meta, and Oracle — up more than sixty percent year over year, with roughly three quarters of that figure going to AI infrastructure. Amazon alone announced $200 billion. Data center infrastructure spending is projected to approach $7 trillion over the next five years. The CHIPS and Science Act has already unlocked more than $500 billion in private semiconductor commitments, tripling domestic chip capacity by 2032. Every dollar of that build-out is a new physical target surface — substation, transformer, cooling array, fiber corridor, fab clean room — that needs protection from day one. And the builders are now the ones paying for it.

$700B
Hyperscaler 2026 combined capex
$7T
Projected 5-year data center infra spend
$500B+
CHIPS Act private commitments unlocked

Sources · CNBC Feb 2026 · Futurum AI Capex 2026 Report · Morgan Lewis Data Center 2026 Outlook · CHIPS.gov / NIST

02 Mar 16, 2026 · FAA Final Rule Target

BVLOS Moves From Exception to Rule

The FAA’s Part 108 final rule on Normalizing UAS Beyond Visual Line of Sight Operations is targeted for publication on March 16, 2026. It replaces the case-by-case BVLOS waiver regime with a standardized operational approvals regime — routine flights in approved operational areas, no per-flight permission, aircraft up to 1,320 pounds. Deus X already holds the only nationwide 400-foot BVLOS waiver on the books. When the waiver becomes an approval, we don’t catch up to the regulation. We were already there.

Mar 2026
Part 108 final rule target
1,320 lbs
Max aircraft weight covered
1 of 1
Nationwide 400ft BVLOS waiver (DxD)

Sources · Federal Register 2025-14992 · FAA.gov BVLOS Newsroom · Pilot Institute

03 2023–2026 · Physical AI Inflection

Autonomy Crossed the Deployment Line

In 2020, drones needed pilots and robots needed teleoperators. Between 2023 and 2026, physical AI crossed the deployment line. NVIDIA released Cosmos world foundation models, Isaac Lab and the Omniverse simulation stack, the GR00T N1.6 reasoning vision-language-action model for humanoids, and the Newton physics engine for sim-to-real transfer — an entire open stack for autonomous physical systems, delivered in twenty-four months. Boston Dynamics, Figure AI, Agility Robotics, Disney Research, Skild AI, Franka, Hexagon, Solomon, and Techman are all building on it. The 1-to-N operator-to-autonomous-system ratio that was science fiction in 2020 is standard operating doctrine in 2026. Deus X does not need to invent the autonomy stack — we inherit it.

2023–26
Physical AI stack arrival window
9+
Robotics leaders on Isaac · Omniverse
1 : N
Operator-to-autonomous-system ratio

Sources · NVIDIA Newsroom 2025 · The Robot Report (Newton · GR00T CoRL 2025) · Commercial UAV News 2026

04 May 2026 · CISA Final Rule

Three Hundred Thousand Entities Just Got a 72-Hour Clock

CISA’s final CIRCIA rule publishes in May 2026. It requires critical infrastructure operators — more than three hundred thousand entities across sixteen sectors — to report significant cyber incidents within 72 hours and ransomware payments within 24. The rule is written for cyber, but the attack surface is physical too: a drone strike on a water plant, a substation, a data-center cooling array — every one of those is a reportable incident. Reporting requirements force detection requirements. Detection requirements force spending. The budget cycle has already started.

300K+
Covered critical infrastructure entities
72 hr
Incident reporting window
24 hr
Ransomware payment reporting

Sources · CISA Regulatory Agenda · CyberScoop · IAPP · Davis Wright Tremaine

05 2026–2030 · NSSE Calendar

Three Deadlines Nobody Can Move

FIFA World Cup 2026 (eleven U.S. host cities, summer 2026). America 250 — the semiquincentennial, July 2026. LA Olympics 2028. Each is a National Special Security Event or Special Event Assessment Rating 1 event. Each has federal C-UAS grant funding already allocated to its host jurisdictions. Each has a date that cannot slip. Deus X’s verbal dev-and-launch partnership with LAPD was made for exactly this calendar. The selection cycle for the 2026–2028 window is running now — not in 2027.

$625M
FEMA FIFA 2026 C-UAS grant program
11
U.S. host cities receiving dedicated funding
LAPD
DxD dev-and-launch partner for LA28

Sources · DHS Dec 30, 2025 Announcement · FEMA NOFO · FY26 NDAA (S.3481)

06 Dec 18, 2025 · Public Law · FY26 NDAA

State and Local Hands Are Finally Unbound

Congress passed the SAFER SKIES Act on December 18, 2025 as part of the FY26 National Defense Authorization Act. For the first time in U.S. history, state, local, tribal, and territorial law enforcement — and correctional agencies — have a statutory path to detect, track, seize, disable, or destroy drones that threaten people, critical infrastructure, or major public events. The authority that was federal-only in 2020 just became local in 2026.

$1B+
Federal C-UAS funding authorized (2026–27)
$500M
FEMA C-UAS grant program (two year)
5 actions
Detect · track · seize · disable · destroy

Sources · S.3481 (Congress.gov) · FEMA NOFO · DHS Announcement Dec 30, 2025

07 Aug 2025 · SecDef Directive

The Pentagon Stood Up a Counter-Drone Command

In August 2025, Secretary of Defense Hegseth directed the establishment of Joint Interagency Task Force 401 under Army lead. Its director reports directly to the Deputy Secretary of Defense and is authorized to allocate up to fifty million dollars per initiative for counter-small-UAS programs. The pre-existing Joint C-sUAS Office is being disestablished. The federal command structure for physical airspace defense is being rebuilt around a single unified authority — one that did not exist in any form in 2020. Deus X already operates inside this command’s orbit.

$50M
Per-initiative allocation authority
Aug 2025
JIATF-401 stood up
Dep SecDef
Direct reporting line

Sources · DoD establishment memo Aug 2025 · Breaking Defense · Federal News Network

In 2020, none of these were true. By 2026, all seven are. The window is open — and it has a close date.

The Case for 2026
Sovereign OS

One OS. Two Portfolios.
One Closed Loop.

Every competitor sells a point product. We built the operating system. PUPPETMASTER protects the physical environment. COMMAND proves compliance. Sovereign OS connects them — closing a detect–respond–report–learn loop that no competitor has.

SOVEREIGN OS — Unified Console
PUPPETMASTER
Protect
Physical security — drones, sensors, robots, access. One platform to command every asset in real time.
AIR
UAS command & control, drone-as-first-responder, HALO counter-drone. Detection to defeat in a single autonomous loop. Only nationwide BVLOS waiver at 400ft.
Live Build
HALO — Air Defense
Kinetic counter-drone intercept. $30K/unit. Three-layer kill chain. GPS-denied capable.

The Closed Loop — No Competitor Has All Four
01
Sense
Drones, sensors, and operators detect threats in real time
02
Respond
PUPPETMASTER dispatches autonomous or human response
03
Prove

04

Every incident makes SENTINEL smarter — for every site on the platform
Palantir
Intelligence only. No physical response. No CI compliance automation.
Anduril
Warfighter systems. No CIRCIA reporting. No manned operator base.
Dedrone / DroneShield
Air only. No compliance. No ground. No closed loop.
Deus X Defense
Detect. Respond. Prove. Learn. The only full-stack CI protection + compliance platform.
From Point Products to Platform

Five Steps to
Sovereign OS.

We don’t wait to have everything built before we sell. Each step generates revenue, proves the model, and unlocks the next. The foundation is complete. We are here — step two. The machine is already running.

Phase 1
Point Products
Each module solves a real problem and generates revenue on its own. Customers buy a solution, not a vision. Revenue today — platform tomorrow.
Steps 1–2
→
Phase 2
Connect & Compound
Modules begin sharing data. PUPPETMASTER ops feed COMMAND compliance. Cross-sell expands contract value. Switching costs compound with every filing cycle.
Steps 3–4
→
Phase 3
Unified Platform
Sovereign OS orchestrates everything. Sense → Decide → Act → Report + Learn as one autonomous loop. Every deployment makes every other deployment smarter.
Step 5
1
Complete
Build the Operational Foundation
Acquire profitable manned security firms. Inherit their revenue, operators, CI relationships, and on-site access. Solve the cold start problem that kills pure-play robotics companies — build a cash-flowing business that funds the technology of tomorrow.
Cascadia — Acquired Invictus — Closing ~$25M Revenue Base 450+ Operators on CI Sites
Unlocked
Live environments at Kinder Morgan, Chevron, and confidential energy majors — no cold calls required. The operators came with the acquisition. The relationships came with the operators.
2
We Are Here
Dominate the Air
Lead with the hardest differentiation. Drone-as-First-Responder and counter-UAS are the products that prove the platform works in real, high-stakes environments. Every flight generates the proprietary low-altitude CI dataset that competitors cannot replicate.
AIR MVP — Live HALO cUAS — Next · June 2026 LAPD Phase 1 — Signing Confidential Hyperscale Partner — Active BVLOS Authorization — 400ft Nationwide
Next up
April – May 2026
Deus X Defense + LAPD launch nation’s most advanced DFR & cUAS program — setting the standard for urban airspace management ahead of the 2028 Olympics. Every law enforcement agency in the country will watch this deployment.
“PUPPETMASTER isn’t a drone product — it’s the air traffic control layer for critical infrastructure. One operator, many platforms, every airspace rule handled automatically.” — Patrick Madden, CEO
Unlocks: Proprietary CI airspace dataset · Regulatory credibility · DaaS revenue · On-site trust for every product that follows
3
Next
Prove Compliance Value
COMMAND goes live. Sensor data from PUPPETMASTER automatically flows into regulatory filings, compliance scoring, and audit evidence. This closes the detect–respond–report loop — and triggers the switching cost that makes every customer permanent.
COMMAND MVP — Q3 2026 CIRCIA Mandate — May 2026 CIP Compliance IP — LOI Signed Accenture Distribution — Signed
Upcoming
Q3 2026 — ahead of CIRCIA mandate
Deus X Defense launches COMMAND — the only platform that automatically files CIRCIA 72-hour reports from live sensor data. 300,000+ covered entities. Mandatory. No other vendor closes this loop.
“CIRCIA compliance will be mandatory for all 16 CI sectors. COMMAND automates 80% of the reporting burden on day one.” — Patrick Madden, CEO
Unlocks: Compliance pipe between CI operators and federal regulators · Switching costs deepen with every filing cycle · SaaS economics
4
Building
Wire the Nervous System
NERVE edge sensors go into the physical infrastructure. Cameras, radar, RF, acoustic — embedded at the facility level. Once hardware is installed inside a customer’s walls, switching vendors means ripping out the building’s nervous system.
SITE Module — Q4 2026 NERVE Sensor IP — LOI Imminent Dallas ISD NERVE Install — Next Confidential Hyperscale — Designing In
Upcoming
Q4 2026
Dallas ISD selects Deus X Defense for district-wide sensor infrastructure — ahead of $250M bond vote. The blueprint for every next-generation school in America.
“This is the inflection point. Two products that sold independently now act as one. That’s the pattern for everything that follows.” — Patrick Madden, CEO
Unlocks: Hardware-level switching costs · Persistent site intelligence · NERVE becomes part of the facility itself
5
Vision
Activate the Immune System
All modules unified. SENTINEL AI learns continuously from every site, every incident, every operator decision. The platform becomes autonomous — detecting threats, responding, filing compliance reports, and improving without human intervention. Every customer makes every other customer safer.
Full Sovereign OS — 2027 GROUND Module — 2027 Virtual RedCell AI — 2027+ Cross-Site Intelligence — H1 2027
The goal
H2 2027
Deus X Defense deploys full Sovereign OS for LA28 Olympics — the most watched security operation in the world. Every CI operator watching will want what LAPD has.
“Not a collection of products — a living immune system that gets smarter every day.” — Patrick Madden, CEO
The result: A platform that gets smarter for every customer with every deployment · Network effects that no competitor can replicate with capital alone
How We Win

Build It on Ourselves.
Get In With What They Need.
Earn the Rest.

We don’t ask customers to buy the vision. We solve the problem in front of them today — a drone contract, a compliance mandate, an operator upgrade — and we expand from that beachhead. The thesis isn’t a plan. It’s already playing out.

Layer 01 — How We Build
01
Internal Ops First
Every module is deployed on our own 450 operators before a customer ever sees it. Real environments. Real threats. Real edge cases. We don’t ship vaporware — we ship battle-tested product.
→
02 — We Are Here
MVP With Partner
Development partners co-deploy the MVP in live environments. They fund the R&D. They generate immediate revenue. They unlock non-dilutive federal capital. We build in production, not a lab.
→
03
GA + Expand
General availability launches with a proven product, a reference customer, and operational data competitors can’t replicate. Every GA unlocks the next module in the customer’s account.
Layer 02 — How We Get In
Entry Point 01
Airspace / DaaS
Customer needs a drone program. We bring operators, hardware, and AIR command & control. They get outcomes on day one.
DaaS contract — revenue from day one
HALO counter-drone added to contract
SITE sensors + COMMAND compliance layer
Full Sovereign OS deployment
Entry Point 02
Compliance / CIRCIA
CIRCIA mandate hits May 2026. 300K+ entities need automated 72-hr incident reporting. COMMAND is purpose-built for exactly this.
COMMAND REPORT — CIRCIA filing automated
EVIDENCE + POSTURE modules expand
PUPPETMASTER data closes the physical loop
Full Sovereign OS deployment
Entry Point 03
Manned Upgrade
Existing security operator relationships. We already have operators on-site at the most sensitive CI in the country. We inject the technology layer into what we already own.
Manned guarding — operators on-site now
PUPPETMASTER C2 injected into operations
Autonomous systems reduce headcount need
Full Sovereign OS — margin 5% → 30%+
Layer 03 — The Thesis Playing Out
THE WORK LEFT TO DO: MISSION VALIDATION IN FLIGHT
Public Safety — Largest Municipal DFR Program in the US
LAPD
Drone-as-First-Responder & cUAS → Sovereign OS
LIVE // OLYMPIC BLUEPRINT

Live in May with robotic security pilots for World Cup 2026 (DFR/c-UAS). This engagement sets the definitive security standard for the 2028 Los Angeles Olympics.

Dallas ISD
Airspace → NERVE Sensors → $7B Pipeline

Every Beachhead Becomes a Platform.
Get in with what they need today — earn the full deployment over time
The Moat

Why This Compounds

The platform is powerful. The operators make it unbeatable.

Most security technology companies sell hardware or software and walk away. DxD deploys operators. Those operators run the systems 24/7 — and every action they take, every threat they respond to, every edge case they handle becomes training data. We don’t just deploy a platform at your site. We deploy a team that teaches our AI exactly what threats look like at your site. By the time you’re ready for full autonomy, SENTINEL already knows your environment better than any competitor ever could.

The Operator Flywheel
01 — Operators
Run the Systems
DxD staff operate 24/7 — monitoring, responding, handling edge cases. Customers buy outcomes, not hardware to manage.
→
02 — Annotators
Train the AI
In-house annotators label live operational data — feeding SENTINEL’s training loop with site-specific threat signatures no competitor can replicate.
→
03 — Maintenance
Keep Hardware Sharp
Field crews handle battery cycles, firmware, calibration, and repairs. Zero burden on the customer. Zero data degradation.

“Same company runs ops, trains AI, maintains hardware: one closed loop. Every sensor event refines SENTINEL’s threat models. The platform gets smarter for your environment over time.”

Mutually Reinforcing Regulatory Products (MURPs)

Each product deepens the value of every other. The longer a customer stays, the harder it becomes to leave.

01
PUPPETMASTER generates the data that feeds COMMAND. Every sensor log and operator action flows into automated CIRCIA reports and insurance evidence packages.
02
COMMAND makes PUPPETMASTER stickier. Once filings are automated, switching vendors risks non-compliance with federal mandates. Lock-in compounds every quarter.
03
CIRCIA mandates create non-discretionary buyers. 300K+ entities must report. The regulation doesn’t ask — it compels. Every new sector under CIRCIA is an inbound buyer.
04
COMMAND evidence lowers insurance premiums — which funds more deployments. Automated compliance documentation lowers insurer risk scores. Reduced premiums free capital for expanded deployments.
05
Every new site makes every other site smarter. SENTINEL learns cross-site threat patterns. A signature at a nuclear facility immediately sharpens detection at a data center. The network compounds.
Every deployment makes the
next one harder to replace.
The moat deepens automatically
The Team

Operators, Not Theorists.

The question investors ask most: how does a company nine months old land LAPD, a global hyperscaler, and a nuclear operator simultaneously — while building a platform no one else has? The answer isn’t capital. It’s people. This team has already built the prior generation of every product DxD is commercializing. They know the customers, the regulators, and the threat environment from the inside. They’ve operated in harder rooms than a boardroom.
Cluster 01 — The Founders
The operators who built the prior generation of drones, robotics, command & control — and convened to build the next one.
PM
Patrick Madden
Founder & CEO
Marine Force Recon. SVP & GM TASER & Robotics at Axon — led TASER 10, Sky-Hero acquisition, and Axon’s DFR/cUAS pivot. McKinsey. Wharton MBA.
NG
Nick Gilmour
Head of Product
Built DFR for Skydio. The product every police department deploys now. Software at Axon and GoPro. Knows what the customer needs before they ask.
DP
Dean Pratt
AI Architecture
Former Accenture AI Architect. Expert in scaling enterprise-grade AI frameworks and integration.
BF
Brendan Flannery
Intelligence & Systems
Architect of Mission-Critical Autonomous Systems with deep US Intelligence Community expertise.
Cluster 02 — We Own the Drone Operation and Regulatory Space
The people who wrote the DFR playbook, shaped the FAA framework, and set the training standards that every agency now follows. They didn’t study the rules — they wrote them.
RR
Rob Robertson
Regulatory & Compliance
FAA liaison who shaped the BVLOS framework for DFR. Implemented DFR programs across multiple agencies. NFL, MLB, NCAA UAS lead. The reason DxD holds authorizations others can’t get.
JB
Jon Beal
Training
Founded the Law Enforcement Drone Association. Wrote the training standards every agency references. Career law enforcement. He defined what "qualified" means in this space.
RS
Ross Stinson
Drone Operations
Built the nation’s largest police drone unit. 20 years law enforcement. Multi-agency UAS task force lead. The operational credibility no hire can manufacture.
Cluster 03 — Warfighters Who Execute
The operators, investigators, and executives who have run harder operations than a sales cycle.
ME
Mike Egan
Chief Financial Officer
U.S. Army Green Beret. JP Morgan. Southwest Airlines. Carvana. Multi-startup executive. Wharton MBA.
WA
Ward Ault
Chief of Staff
Marine Force Recon. Chief of Staff to Axon CEO — ran ops alongside Madden. PE-backed startup CEO before DxD. Harvard MBA.
KH
Kevin Hill
Partnerships
Former Microsoft & IBM Commercial Executive Leader. Enterprise sales and partnership strategy at global scale. Connecting DxD to the distribution channels that matter.
TR
Tesh Rao
Business Development
Naval Aviator. 250+ combat missions. Senior Aide to the Supreme Allied Commander, Europe (NATO). Opens doors at NATO and the federal level that no BD hire can reach.
Advisory Board — The Network That Opens Doors
Not decorative. These advisors actively open doors, shape standards, and provide access to networks that define the future of this space.
WB
William P. Barr
77th & 85th U.S. Attorney General · 6 years CIA · Led national security interagency coordination · General Counsel, Verizon/GTE. The highest law enforcement credential available to a private company.
JS
TBC
Adm. James Stavridis
Former Supreme Allied Commander, NATO · Advisor, Carlyle Group · Maritime security & homeland defense. NATO relationships no competitor can access.
MI
TBC
Chief Mike IGO
Former Chief of Dallas PD · Public safety leadership at national scale · Direct relationships across the municipal and federal law enforcement community.
AD
Aaditya Devarakonda
Founder & CEO, DeDrone · CNBC Disruptor 50 · Built the category DxD is expanding. The person who defined commercial counter-drone is now advising DxD.
AL
Adam Lee
VP & Chief Security Officer, Dominion Energy · 22-year FBI career, former Special Agent in Charge, Richmond · The bridge between enterprise CI security and the federal threat picture.
+
Additional Advisors
Former intelligence agency director · Former Allied Universal board chair · Additional confidential advisors pending public announcement. The network runs deeper than what’s listed here.
This network doesn’t just open doors.
It is the doors.
Traction

The Market Found Us.

~$25M in acquired revenue. $105M+ in qualified pipeline. No dedicated sales team. Every major deal came inbound or through a partner. The thesis isn’t theoretical — it’s already playing out.

~$25M
Revenue Base
Cascadia + Invictus — acquired, cash-flowing from day one
$105M+
Qualified pipeline sourced via 100% pull-demand for DFR/DaaS only. Excludes Accenture, Deloitte (pending), and cUAS potential. Manned guarding revenue is tracked in a separate services pipeline.
Qualified Pipeline
No outbound sales — inbound and partner-sourced
3
Development Partners
LAPD · Confidential Hyperscale · Energy sector imminent
Enterprise & Partners
Accenture GTM signed — GSOC verbally committed. Teamed on LAPD. Generated LADWP unsolicited RFP. One partnership, three doors opened.
One of world’s three largest consulting firms — inbound to hand off drone training operations. World Cup 2026 operating partner discussion active.
Confidential Executive Client — $500K ARR, live. Expanding to full corporate campus.
Confidential Pipeline Operator — DFR demo identified a live security gap their team had missed. Within 48 hours: “Can you do our guarding too?” $15–20M manned + $5–10M DaaS proposal submitted.
Government & Public Sector
LAPD Phase 1 signing in progress. Joint Accenture+DxD roadmap. 3-phase citywide autonomous command. $5–9M annual savings per site quantified.
LAPD → LADWP unsolicited RFP. Scope: LA28 Olympics + NERC CIP. We didn’t pitch LADWP. LAPD impressed them for us.
Dallas ISD — $7.5M sole-source DFR RFP. Named by specification. Award in process.
Dallas ISD $250M bond — district-wide sensor retrofit of existing schools. Q3 vote. DxD positioned as primary infrastructure vendor.
Dallas ISD $7B new construction pipeline — every new school built with Sovereign OS sensor architecture from day one.
Richmond County Sheriff’s Office — pilot underway by end of April. South Carolina DoC following.
Energy & Infrastructure
Confidential Energy Major — $1M ARR manned guarding, inherited via Cascadia. Now requesting DaaS expansion. Zero prospecting required.
Confidential Hyperscale Partner — $25K/mo DFR beachhead. Expanding to cUAS. Next-phase data centers designed with Sovereign OS built in from day one.
Third development partner slot — energy sector imminent. Targeting critical energy generation, transmission, and data center operators. Multiple in conversation.

The market is pulling us.
Every major deal came inbound or through a partner
Appendix C

Airspace Advantage

The Airspace Advantage
DxD Is Writing the Rules for Urban Airspace
The sub-400ft airspace is about to become the most contested real estate in America. VTOL air taxis. Amazon Prime Air. Commercial delivery drones. All of them are coming — all below 400ft — all needing to coexist with law enforcement and security operations. DxD is co-authoring the deconfliction architecture for Los Angeles in partnership with LAPD — the largest police department in the country. One of fewer than 200 entities in the U.S. authorized for BVLOS at 400ft — the only private company doing so for critical infrastructure security at scale.
LAPD DFR Reserved — 200ft+ AGL
Geofenced floor enforced in Skydio C2. Descends only on active escalation. LAPD assumes full airspace authority.
DxD Day Respond — 100–150ft AGL
Security operations, daytime. Skydio X10, parachute required, above roofline. Speaker + lights.
DxD Night Respond — 50–150ft AGL
Tighter proximity. Minimal foot traffic after hours. Full lower envelope for speaker deterrence.
DxD Patrol — 25–50ft AGL
Persistent 24/7 monitoring. Holds at 50ft or below when LAPD DFR is active.
Escalation SOP — When LAPD DFR enters working altitude, DxD yields and resumes. This deconfliction framework becomes the template for every major American city.

Appendix B — Key Model Assumptions
Conservative base case — multiple revenue streams at $0
Manned Guarding
  • •  TTM $25M → $35M in FY26 (40% growth via cross-sell + pricing)
  • •  Gross margin: 8% → 14% through operational efficiencies
  • •  5% quarterly revenue growth in 2027; margin → 18% by Q4’27
DaaS — Sunflower
  • •  $9,000/dock/month as-a-service; 1:4 operator ratio
  • •  M&A upsell: 1 customer/month from June ’26 (2 docks each)
  • •  Enterprise small deals: 1 per month (EP/events)
DaaS — Skydio
  • •  100% Skydio for public sector DFR; 4 docks per deal
  • •  Operator ratio 1:2 in 2026 (conservative); 1:4 in 2027
  • •  Enterprise mix: 25% Skydio in H1’26 → 50% by year-end
cUAS (Orcrist / HALO)
  • •  $30K MSRP, $15K COGS (50% HW margin); $1K/unit/mo SW license
  • •  Ramp: 0 → 700 units in 2026; 6,900 in 2027
  • •  10% sales commission (direct); 20% distributor discount
Operating Expenses
  • •  AI-optimized lean model: contractor-heavy, minimal fixed overhead
  • •  Eng + R&D grow 10%/mo in ’26, 25%/qtr in ’27 (invest posture)
  • •  EBITDA-positive monthly by Q4’26; >25% target at scale
Conservative Guardrails ($0)
  • •  Accenture / hyperscaler channel revenue: $0
  • •  PUPPETMASTER C2 SaaS revenue: $0
  • •  UGV / ground robotics revenue: $0
  • •  No single “whale” deal assumed in base case

Appendix B

User Stories

■ USE CASE 01
LAPD — Urban Airspace Deconfliction & DFR

The Los Angeles Police Department requires real-time drone-as-first-responder capability across a complex urban airspace environment ahead of the 2026 World Cup. DxD’s PUPPETMASTER AIR module provides unified command & control, BVLOS authorization at 400ft nationwide, and the only integrated airspace deconfliction layer purpose-built for law enforcement. One operator. Many platforms. Every airspace rule handled automatically.

▶ STATUS: PENDING — DECISION NOT YET MADE. TRENDING POSITIVE.
■ USE CASE 02
Dallas ISD — $7B Pipeline & NERVE Sensor Retrofit

Dallas Independent School District is building the next generation of physical security infrastructure across its campus portfolio. DxD’s NERVE edge sensor platform — cameras, radar, RF, and acoustic arrays — embeds at the facility level, creating hardware-level switching costs and persistent site intelligence. The $7B pipeline represents the district’s broader infrastructure modernization, with DxD positioned as the integrated security OS layer across all future builds.

▶ STATUS: PENDING — DECISION NOT YET MADE. TRENDING POSITIVE.
■ USE CASE 03
Caris Life Sciences / Founder — HNW to Corporate Campus Security

A high-net-worth founder and life sciences executive requires integrated personal and corporate campus security. DxD’s unified platform bridges HNW executive protection and enterprise perimeter defense — combining PUPPETMASTER drone command, NERVE sensor deployment, and SENTINEL AI threat intelligence into a single managed service. This profile represents a rapidly expanding cross-sell vector: individuals who control both personal security spend and enterprise procurement decisions.

▶ STATUS: PENDING — DECISION NOT YET MADE. TRENDING POSITIVE.
Overview
CORTEX
PUPPETMASTER
COMMAND
HALO
Sovereign OS

The Security Immune System

A unified command platform that combines manned guarding, autonomous drones, edge AI, and counter-UAS into one integrated security operation. Not a product. A system that learns, responds, and heals itself.

CORTEX — AI Sense Layer PUPPETMASTER — C2 Platform COMMAND — Compliance Engine HALO — Air Defense
System Architecture

Sovereign OS — Three-Tier Platform

The Sovereign OS is a three-tier architecture: a unified platform core at the center, five capability layers that process and act on data, and four domain modules that control physical assets. Every asset — guard, drone, sensor, interceptor — connects to one command surface.

Pillar One — AI Sense
CORTEX
Detect · Fuse · Alert
Edge AI that processes camera feeds, sensors, and behavioral data into actionable alerts. Connects to any sensor via ONVIF/API. Powered by Atlas IED partnership — 95% of US schools already run this hardware.
Live — Revenue Generating
Pillar Two — C2 Platform
PUPPETMASTER
Command · Deploy · Defend
Single pane of glass that orchestrates guards, drones, sensors, and cUAS. Hardware-agnostic fleet abstraction — one dispatch command regardless of manufacturer. The abstraction layer is the moat.
Live — DaaS Revenue Active
Pillar Three — Compliance
COMMAND
Report · Posture · Verify
Automated compliance engine that turns operational data into audit-ready reports. Every incident, every decision, every response — structured and attributed in real-time. Eliminates the compliance burden entirely.
Next — MVP Q2 2026
Pillar Four — Air Defense
HALO
Sense · Slew · Strike
Three-layer integrated air defense: Oracle (C2), Machina (autonomous slew-to-cue), Orcrist SEAM-1 (kinetic intercept). $30K/unit. Exclusive commercial rights. The only commercially available hard-kill cUAS at this price point.
Next — Deploying with LAPD
The Margin Engine

Guard force is the distribution channel. Technology is the margin engine.

Manned Only
8%
EBITDA
Tech-Enhanced
14%
EBITDA
DaaS
35%
Gross Margin
Autonomous
50–60%
Target GM
Pillar One

CORTEX — AI Sense Layer

Edge AI that processes every camera feed, sensor, and behavioral signal into a unified intelligence picture. Not a VMS. Not a PSIM. A physical security intelligence engine that turns raw sensor data into actionable decisions.

Live — Revenue Generating ONVIF + API Integration Atlas IED Partnership
Architecture

CORTEX — Intelligence Pipeline

CORTEX ingests sensor data at the edge, processes it through AI models, and outputs structured intelligence to PUPPETMASTER and COMMAND. The pipeline is designed for real-time response — detection to alert in under 2 seconds.

Pricing Tiers

Priced per site. Not per camera.

Standard
Up to 48 feeds
Compact edge appliance. Individual CI sites, substations, single buildings. Full AI stack — same models, same correlation, same SENTINEL integration. The difference is throughput capacity, not capability.
Professional
Up to 128 feeds
Higher compute, larger buffer. Mid-size campuses, multi-building facilities. Extended 7-day investigation buffer standard. This is the sweet spot for most CI customers — enough capacity for campus-wide coverage with room to grow.
Enterprise
Up to 512 feeds
Rack-mounted. Large campuses, multi-site aggregation. 30-day pattern recognition buffer standard. Enterprise CORTEX can aggregate feeds from multiple Standard units — turning a distributed sensor network into a single intelligence picture.
The Bundled Model
CORTEX + operators + drones + COMMAND. One price: "DxD protects your site." DxD doesn't sell hardware and leave. It deploys a complete security operation — the technology, the trained operators who know how to run it, and the accountability for whether the site is actually protected.
Atlas IED Partnership
95% of US schools run Atlas IED/Mitek hardware. That installed base represents an upgrade-ready market for CORTEX-integrated sensors — schools that already trust the manufacturer and already have the mounting infrastructure in place. DxD controls the full stack from silicon to compliance report.
Pillar Two

PUPPETMASTER — C2 Platform

Single pane of glass command and control. Guards, drones, sensors, cUAS — one dispatch command, one airspace picture, one accountability chain. The hardware abstraction layer is the core technical moat.

Live — DaaS Revenue Active 7 Drone Platforms BVLOS 400ft Nationwide
Architecture

PUPPETMASTER — Fleet Abstraction Layer

PUPPETMASTER abstracts heterogeneous hardware behind a single dispatch interface. The operator clicks "respond" — PUPPETMASTER selects the right platform based on mission profile, distance, weather, airspace, and availability. A competitor can buy the same drones. They can't replicate the abstraction layer.

Four Modules

One orchestration layer. Four operational domains.

AIR
Live
UAS command and control, drone-as-first-responder, BVLOS operations, airspace deconfliction. The beachhead module. Already generating revenue through DaaS contracts. Every DaaS contract is a wedge for the full platform.
HALO — Aerial Kill Chain
Next
Three layers: Oracle (detection) → Machina (AI-assisted classification) → Orcrist (kinetic intercept). $30K/unit, reusable, GPS-denied capable. The only commercially available hard-kill interceptor at this price point. DxD holds exclusive commercial rights.
SITE
Building
Edge AI sensors, radar, RF, optical, IoT — fused into a single fixed-site awareness layer. Powered by DxD's own sensor platform via the Atlas IED partnership. Purpose-built cameras, gunshot detection, weapons detection, behavioral AI.
GROUND
2027
UGV autonomous patrol and human-machine teaming for perimeter defense. Boston Dynamics Spot, Ghost Robotics — interior, hazardous, or no-fly environments. Same dispatch workflow as AIR. Same data pipeline into CORTEX and COMMAND.
Operational Maturity

Three phases of autonomy progression.

PHASE 01
Operator
Current
"Human directs everything. Every decision captured."
The operator dispatches every drone, acknowledges every alert, directs every response. Every action is logged and labeled. 450+ operators are building the most defensible training corpus in physical security — not synthetic data in a lab, but real security at real sites against real threats. The operators aren't a cost center. They're the training data engine.
Guarding: 8% EBITDA Tech-Enhanced: 14%
PHASE 02
Director
Next
"Human sets the goal. SENTINEL executes."
The operator says "investigate east perimeter." SENTINEL selects the drone, plans the route, adjusts sensors, streams the feed. Phase 2 is the force multiplier. One operator, assisted by SENTINEL, covers the territory that previously required three. Not by cutting corners — by eliminating the mechanical overhead that consumed 60% of an operator's attention.
DaaS: 35% GM
PHASE 03
Commander
2027+
"SENTINEL detects and responds within pre-authorized playbooks."
Routine threats handled autonomously. Patrol adjustments made in real-time. Operators handle edge cases and authorization decisions. This is the margin inflection point — one operator meaningfully covers multiple sites because the system handles predictable responses autonomously. The playbooks are governed by COMMAND's DOCTRINE module — versioned, auditable, revocable.
Autonomous: 50–60% GM target
Pillar Three

COMMAND — Compliance Engine

Automated compliance that turns operational data into audit-ready reports. Every incident, every decision, every response — structured and attributed in real-time. The compliance report doesn't need to be written because it was being assembled as the incident unfolded.

MVP Q2 2026 Auto-Generated Reports CJIS Certified Operators
Architecture

COMMAND — Four-Module Compliance Stack

COMMAND is organized into four modules that together eliminate the compliance burden: REPORT (auto-generated incident documentation), POSTURE (real-time security posture scoring), VERIFY (third-party audit readiness), and EVIDENCE (chain-of-custody management).

The Compliance Moat
The switching cost isn't the hardware. It's everything the hardware generates. A customer can buy the same drones elsewhere. They can't replicate 18 months of operational learning, compliance records, and SENTINEL training data. COMMAND's compliance records constitute the official history of the site's security operations — and that history only appreciates in value.
Pillar Four

HALO — Integrated Air Defense

Three-layer kill chain: Oracle (sense), Machina (slew), Orcrist (strike). The only commercially available hard-kill cUAS interceptor at a $30K price point. DxD holds exclusive global commercial distribution rights.

Deploying with LAPD $30K / Unit — Reusable Exclusive Commercial Rights
Kill Chain Architecture

HALO IADS — Sense · Slew · Strike

The Deus Halo IADS is a completely decentralized air defense ecosystem. It gives a single dismounted squad the unjammable airspace denial of a legacy Patriot battery, for a fraction of the cost. Three layers, no single point of failure.

Cost Exchange Ratio — Operation Epic Fury
$2,000 : $2,000,000
Orcrist intercept cost vs. legacy interceptor cost per engagement
Market Position

22% of TYTAN production target — conservative positioning in a demand-constrained market.

TYTAN (GERMANY)

Bundeswehr contract
DEUS X (ORCRIST)
~667/mo
Q1 2027 target — 22% of TYTAN
Exclusive Commercial Rights
DxD holds exclusive global commercial distribution rights for Orcrist SEAM-1. Where DroneShield jams and Dedrone detects, Orcrist kills. The complete kill chain — sense, decide, destroy — under one roof. For domestic operations: capture and controlled landing. For defense applications: full kinetic defeat. Same architecture. Different rules of engagement.
© 2026 Deus X Defense, Inc. — Confidential Product Architecture — Do Not Distribute

Financial Projections

Working Financial Model — Directional Only
This model reflects our current operating plan as of April 2026. It is directional, not a formal projection. DxD operates in a fast-moving market with rapidly evolving partnerships, pipeline, and product timelines — this model is updated continuously as conditions change. Key revenue lines are modeled conservatively: multiple identified revenue streams are held at $0 pending contract execution. Treat these numbers as a working framework for how we think about the business, not a commitment.
Summary P&L
Revenue Drivers
Sensitivity
Assumptions
Unit Economics

What happens if key assumptions don't hit plan? Each scenario below is derived from the base case model — adjusted for a single variable change to isolate impact.

Brief A · Investment Committee · April 2026

Deus Halo IADS & Counter-UAS Market Primer

Three-Layer Kill Chain

LAYER 01
SENSE
Halo C2 “Oracle”
Single pane of glass, ATAK plugin, AES-256 encrypted MAVLink 2.0
LAYER 02
SLEW
M-ALS “Machina”
Autonomous slew-to-cue, multi-modal mobility, voice commands
LAYER 03
STRIKE
SEAM-1 “Orcrist”
$30K per unit, NATO-sourced COTS, IP-67, <5 min operator training

Validated Demand Signal

Pentagon FY2026
$3.1B
Dedicated C-UAS budget. JIATF-401 directive mandates drone interceptor procurement across all services.
Raytheon Coyote
$5B
Army contract for Coyote interceptors. Demand exceeds production capacity at every tier.
Anduril Bolt
175+/mo
Target production rate for 2026. Reusable VTOL kinetic interceptor.
DxD Orcrist Target
~667/mo
Q2 2027 steady-state. 22% of TYTAN rate — conservative positioning in a demand-constrained market.

Brief B · 26 partners · 7 MOUs signed

Partnership Ecosystem

DxD’s moat is a stack of partners that each compress one of the four frictions blocking competitors: regulatory, operational, deployment, and integration.

Distribution & Go-to-Market

Accenture
8,000+
Enterprise client access through existing trust relationships. Moves customers from Security Budget to Capital Plan. Live Q3 ’26.
LAPD · World Cup ’26
Public Sector
“If you’re successful, we’ll parade you around the world.” Sets the public-sector reference standard.
Orcrist (Exclusive)
$30K
Only commercially available hard-kill cUAS interceptor at this price point. DxD holds exclusive global commercial distribution rights.

Cascadia Global Security
Closed
Adds revenue, geographic reach, and guard force for the DaaS upsell engine.
Holdfast
Closed
Expands the manned-guarding base — the distribution channel for autonomous leverage.
Invictus International Holdings

Brief C · Quantified outcomes

Case Studies

Deployment outcomes across three verticals: public safety, critical infrastructure, and enterprise distribution.

Public Safety · LAPD
World Cup ’26
Deus Halo air-defense deployment. Deputy Chief: “I’ve seen enough. We need you.” Pilot ended early in DxD’s favor — sets the public-sector reference standard.
Critical Infrastructure
BVLOS @ 400ft
One of fewer than five nationwide BVLOS waivers ever issued at this altitude. 18 months to earn. Enables Drone-as-First-Responder coverage across distributed sites.
Enterprise · via Accenture
Capital Plan
Migrating clients from a recurring Security OpEx line to a multi-year Capital Plan. PUPPETMASTER as the unifying control surface across guards, drones, sensors, and cUAS.
Manned Guarding Base
~$25M TTM
Combined-entity revenue. Low-margin guarding (~7% EBITDA) becomes the distribution channel; PUPPETMASTER layers in >30% GM tech revenue scaling toward 50–60%.
Named-customer case studies with full deployment metrics, contract terms, and reference contacts available under NDA. Request file →
Brief D · Product walkthroughs

Product Demos

Demonstrations and field deployment footage. Live demos available on request.

PUPPETMASTER C2
Live April
Single pane of glass that unifies guards, drones, sensors, and cUAS into one command surface. Operator clicks “respond” — PUPPETMASTER selects the right platform based on mission profile, distance, weather, airspace, and availability.
Deus Halo IADS
3-Layer Kill Chain
Sense (Oracle C2) → Slew (Machina M-ALS) → Strike (Orcrist SEAM-1). Footage of integrated sense-slew-strike sequence against representative threat profiles.
Drone-as-First-Responder
DFR
Autonomous response from rooftop docks under nationwide BVLOS waiver. Removes the human from the point of threat — 30-second arrival vs. 60-second human dispatch.
DaaS Field Operations
In Production
Drone-as-a-Service deployment footage. CJIS-certified law-enforcement operators in the loop. Site-specific training — works in the mud, not just the demo room.
Video links and live demo scheduling available on request. Schedule walkthrough →
